title: Houses for Rent in Beachwood, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Beachwood, Ohio
metaDescription: Looking for houses for rent in Beachwood, Ohio? See Cleveland-area rent ranges, housing stock, and transit, then tell us what you need.
answerBox: Beachwood, Ohio is an east Cuyahoga County suburb (ZIP 44122) along I-271 near Beachwood Place and Chagrin Boulevard. Rents across Greater Cleveland typically run $700-$1,800 a month. Beachwood isn't currently in our selection, but tell us your bedroom count and budget and we'll compare nearby options in Cleveland.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Beachwood, Ohio
body: Beachwood is an east Cuyahoga County suburb bordering Shaker Heights to the west and Pepper Pike and Woodmere to the east, with **ZIP code 44122** covering the city. Interstate 271 runs through Beachwood with an interchange at Chagrin Boulevard (U.S. Route 422), and Cedar Road and Richmond Road are additional major corridors. Housing stock ranges from mid-1900s single-family colonials in the city's residential neighborhoods to newer condominium and apartment developments along the Cedar Road and Chagrin Boulevard corridor.

**Rent Finder Cleveland works with 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, and Beachwood is not currently part of our selection.** If you're set on Beachwood specifically, a Beachwood-focused property manager or listing site will have more direct inventory. If your search is flexible on location, [tell us what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) — bedrooms, budget, and whether you hold a housing voucher — and we can point you to comparable options in the Cleveland neighborhoods we do work with.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent in the Cleveland area?
body: Beachwood rents tend to run above the Cleveland city average given its housing mix and proximity to the Chagrin Boulevard commercial corridor, but as a general Greater Cleveland benchmark, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts the metro's median asking rent at **$1,250/month** overall, with 2-bedroom units around **$1,100** and 3-bedroom units around **$1,350**. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which weights toward larger professionally-managed buildings, shows a higher metro average of **$1,564/month** — a figure closer to what Beachwood's condo and apartment stock often reflects. Actual Beachwood house rents will depend on the specific home, its size, and condition.

Beachwood sits along Interstate 271 at the Chagrin Boulevard interchange, with Beachwood Place — a shopping center along Cedar Road near I-271 — serving as one of the city's main commercial anchors. Cedar Road and Richmond Road provide additional north-south and east-west routes through the city. Greater Cleveland RTA's fixed bus routes are concentrated closer to the city and inner-ring suburbs like Shaker Heights, so most Beachwood residents rely on a car for daily transportation.

items: **Highways:** I-271 at the Chagrin Boulevard (US-422) interchange
**Location:** East Cuyahoga County, bordering Shaker Heights, Pepper Pike, and Woodmere
**Commercial district:** Beachwood Place shopping center along Cedar Road

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher holders looking in Beachwood
body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers, and Beachwood is not among the handful of Cuyahoga County communities — Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale — that have adopted local source-of-income protection ordinances, so acceptance in Beachwood depends on the individual landlord. The [Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) administers the countywide Housing Choice Voucher program and accepts preliminary applications year-round. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready, though our current homes are concentrated in Cleveland rather than Beachwood — see our [Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how the program works locally.

If your search spans both Beachwood and Cleveland proper, our [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page covers the neighborhoods where we work with inventory today. You can also compare nearby [Solon](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-solon-ohio/), or [tell us what you need](/p/book-showing) and we'll help you weigh it against homes we do work with.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with houses in Beachwood, Ohio?
faq.a: Not currently. Our 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, and Beachwood is outside our current selection. If you're flexible on location, tell us your bedroom count and budget and we'll point you to comparable options we do work with.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Beachwood, Ohio?
faq.a: Beachwood-specific pricing varies by home and size, but as a Greater Cleveland benchmark, Zumper's July 2026 report shows a metro median of about $1,100 for 2-bedroom and $1,350 for 3-bedroom units, while RentCafe's July 2026 report runs higher for larger professionally-managed buildings.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Beachwood, Ohio in?
faq.a: Beachwood is ZIP code 44122, in east Cuyahoga County, bordering Shaker Heights, Pepper Pike, and Woodmere along Interstate 271.

faq.q: Do Beachwood landlords accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: It depends on the individual landlord. Ohio has no statewide law requiring voucher acceptance, and Beachwood is not among the small number of Cuyahoga County communities with a local source-of-income protection ordinance. CMHA administers the countywide Housing Choice Voucher program.

faq.q: How do I get from Beachwood to downtown Cleveland?
faq.a: Interstate 271 runs through Beachwood and connects toward downtown Cleveland. Greater Cleveland RTA's fixed-route bus network is concentrated closer to the city and inner-ring suburbs, so most Beachwood residents drive for the commute.
